stories.title: New Latvian PM pledges to beef up air defenses
stories.summary: Latvia’s parliament on Thursday voted to appoint businessman-turned-politician Andris Kulbergs as the new prime minister, with his priority being security and anti-drone measures. The 100-seat parliament endorsed Kulbergs’ four-party coalition government by a vote of 66 to 25, demonstrating a strong parliamentary majority comprising the alliance of center-right, conservative and nationalist forces. Speaking before the vote, Kulbergs said Latvia needed “an effective, professional and accountable

stories.title: Spain distances itself from call for EU to get tougher on China
stories.summary: BERLIN — Spain is withdrawing its support from a French-led initiative to boost the EU’s trade defenses against China, its economy and trade minister said on Thursday.  “There was no specific political support of any non-paper at this stage,” Carlos Cuerpo said in Berlin. The minister’s comments mark a reversal of Madrid’s perceived position on tougher trade measures targeting Beijing. Last Friday, Spain was among the large economies who signed onto a French position paper sent

stories.title: EU’s big six reach deal on key markets package
stories.summary: The EU’s six largest countries reached an agreement on Thursday to move forward with a plan to turn the bloc into a Wall Street-style financial market, according to three officials with knowledge of proceedings. The deal struck in Berlin after hours of negotiations is an important step in the decade-old plan to turn the EU into a U.S.-style investment powerhouse. Finance ministers from Germany, France, Spain, Italy, Poland and the Netherlands, known as the “E6,” struck a compromise on key aspect

stories.title: Hungary is on a 'very clear and steadfast' path to restore rule of law, EU justice commissioner says
stories.summary: The comments by the EU’s justice commissioner, Michael McGrath, come ahead of talks between Peter Magyar and Ursula von der Leyen aimed at securing the release of €17 billion in EU funds that were frozen due to concerns over corruption and shortcomings in the rule of law.

stories.title: ‘Saaz is our gold’: the Czech scientists breeding hops that can survive a hotter Europe
stories.summary: Researchers are working to create new drought-resistant varieties of the ingredient that gives Czech pilsner its character It is the country that drinks more beer per capita than any other but in the last few years Czechia has been hit by droughts and heatwaves, which make it harder to grow the Saaz hops, one of the key ingredients that goes into the country’s world famous beer. At the Hop Research Institute, however, scientists are working to create new, climate-resilient hop varieties that hav

stories.title: Russia on the back foot with dynamics of war shifting in Ukraine’s favour, EU says – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Meanwhile, Hungary’s Magyar hits a bit more upbeat tone in his latest post on Facebook, saying that while there are still some issues that need to be resolved, the two sides managed to agree a deal on “many important questions.” In a Facebook video, he takes viewers into a meeting room in Brussels from which he is working on ironing out the final details. “I will obviously not anticipate or prejudge what will be the outcome of that meeting, but a series of meetings has be

stories.title: Priest on trial for sexual assault in Texas had child with Louisiana congregant, prosecutors say
stories.summary: DNA testing proved Anthony Odiong fathered child with woman to whom he had been providing spiritual guidance, authorities say Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Texas prosecutors on Thursday established that a Roman Catholic priest being tried there on charges that he illegally exploited his status as a priest to pursue sex with three spiritually vulnerable congregants had a child with a separate congregant in approximately 2023 – while working outside New Orleans . Meanwhile, the

stories.title: Magyar to formally submit Hungary’s application to join the European Public Prosecutor’s Office
stories.summary: The Hungarian PM and some of his government's ministers are in Brussels for meetings and to work on the unblocking of Hungary's frozen EU funds.

stories.title: To reverse the ‘greenlash’, Europe’s Green parties should embrace Polanski’s boldness | Tarik Abou-Chadi
stories.summary: Be more strident and ambitious, take on economic inequality, and progressive voters will reward you as they have the UK’s Greens Tarik Abou-Chadi is a professor of European politics at the University of Oxford European Green parties have been through a phase of stagnation and crisis in recent years. Long gone seem the days of the “green wave” across Europe. Back in 2019, Green parties secured their best-ever result in the European parliament elections, with 74 seats. In the same year, Green part

stories.title: Tabu vagy játék? Így tanulhatnak a gyerekek a pénzről
stories.summary: Szülőként sokan szeretnénk megtanítani a gyerekeinknek mindazt, amit nekünk senki sem magyarázott el. Csakhogy a pénzről beszélni otthon még ma is sokszor nehéz: nem mindig tudjuk, hogyan és mikor kezdjünk bele könnyedén, szorongás nélkül. Kíváncsiak voltunk, hogyan lehet közelebb vinni a gyerekekhez egy ilyen fontos témát, ezért elolvastuk A láthatatlan kincs című interaktív kalandregényt, ami játékos formában segít beszélgetni a pénzről, spórolásról és döntésekről már kisiskolás kortól.

stories.title: Így épült fel Magyarország pornóbirodalma – és most sorozat lett belőle
stories.summary: Van, amit a kilencvenes évek Magyarországán mindenki tudott, de senki sem mondott ki hangosan.

stories.title: Magyar Péter nemsokára hivatalosan is benyújtja Magyarország csatlakozási szándékát az Európai Ügyészséghez
stories.summary: A kormányfő és miniszterei csütörtökön megérkeztek Brüsszelbe, ahol többek közt az uniós pénzek hazahozataláról tárgyalnak.

stories.title: A 100 ezer forintos iskolakezdési támogatás a régióban is nagynak számítana
stories.summary: Az osztrák, lengyel és román állam is külön támogatja az iskolakezdést, de kisebb összeggel, mint ahogy az új kormány tervezi itthon.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor szerint Hajdu János eltávolítása a TEK éléről komoly kockázatot jelent
stories.summary: Hajdu tizenhat évig volt a Terrorelhárítási Központ főigazgatója, Magyar Péter a héten mentette fel a szolgálatából.

stories.summary: Július 1-től megszűnik az összes parkolóautomata Budapesten, ezt használná ki az önkormányzat.

stories.title: Egy használt Porsche 911 is lehet pénztemető
stories.summary: A kétezres évek elejétől gyártott 911-es Porsche a műszaki zsenialitás mellett konstrukciós hibákat is hordoz, emiatt pedig rosszul öregszik. Ez nem az árán látszik, hanem az akár 4-5 millió forintos javítási költségeken.

stories.title: Demeter Szilárd elismerte, majd lazán átsiklott afölött, hogy a közmédiában tényleg tiltólistán voltak előadók
stories.summary: A Köztér című műsorban csak úgy mellékesen megjegyezte, hogy egy „segédszerkesztő hölgytől” rendszeresen kapott emaileket arról, kiket ne játszanak.

stories.title: Kiszámoltuk, hogyan csökken Magyar Péter fizetése a törvénymódosítás után
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt javaslata vastagon érinti a miniszterelnök pénztárcáját is, júliustól bruttó 5,1 millió forintot vihet haza.

stories.title: Sára Botond a választás másnapján 70 milliót osztott szét a beosztottjai között
stories.summary: Ezt állítja a közigazgatásért felelős miniszter, Lőrincz Viktória, aki kijelentette, a miniszterek és államtitkárok mellett a főispánok végkielégítését sem fizeti ki az új kormány.

stories.title: 3 nappal a választás előtt az Orbán-kormány a Szerencsejáték Zrt. összes eredményének kivételéről döntött
stories.summary: A cég két részletben fizeti majd be az 52 milliárd forintos osztalékot az államkasszába.

stories.title: Megkezdődnek a minisztériumi egyeztetések az önkormányzati érdekszervezetekkel
stories.summary: „A Vidék- és Településfejlesztési Minisztérium partnerként tekint a magyar önkormányzatokra” – írta Lőrincz Viktória miniszter.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a NATO-főtitkárnak: Magyarország nem küld fegyvereket és harci eszközöket az orosz–ukrán háborúba
stories.summary: Mark Rutte pedig tájékoztatta a magyar miniszterelnököt az ankarai NATO-csúcs előkészületeiről.

stories.title: Germany: Alleged leftist militant found guilty of robbery
stories.summary: Daniela Klette, suspected to be one of the last remaining members of the left-wing terrorist group Red Army Faction, has been found guilty for her part in a series of robberies — but more serious charges await.

stories.title: Woman caught after 30 years on the run is jailed for armed robberies
stories.summary: Klette was finally caught in a flat in Berlin in 2024 and put on trial last year.

stories.title: Israeli minister confirms goal of large-scale expulsion of Palestinians from Gaza
stories.summary: Human rights groups and lawyers say ‘voluntary migration’ policy amounts to ethnic cleansing Israel’s defence minister has said he is committed to the ethnic cleansing of Gaza through large-scale migration of Palestinians as part of Israel’s long-term plans for the territory. Israel Katz said the government would implement a plan for large numbers of Palestinians to leave Gaza “at the right time and in the right manner”, in a statement on Wednesday marking the targeted killing of Mohammed Odeh,

stories.title: Alarming surge in suicides among ICE detainees, investigation reveals
stories.summary: AP review finds unprecedented number of suicide deaths as critics attack failures of Trump’s immigration crackdown Brayan Rayo Garzón was distraught. Detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ), he was on his fourth day of isolation in a Missouri jail as he battled the fevers and chills of Covid. His request for mental health treatment had been put off, records show, and staff had forbidden Rayo from making his nightly call to his mother, as a precaution intended to prevent the sprea

stories.title: Israel strikes southern Beirut amid increasing escalation in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: The intensification comes after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced an expansion in the Israeli military's attacks in Lebanon, despite an ongoing ceasefire.

stories.title: ‘Unacceptable’: Brussels rebukes Turkey for excluding Cyprus from climate meetings
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission on Thursday castigated Turkey for freezing Cyprus out of preparatory meetings ahead of this year’s United Nations climate conference, calling Ankara’s behavior “unacceptable.” As POLITICO reported Wednesday , the Turkish government — which will host the COP31 summit in Antalya in November — has excluded Cyprus from several informal preparation meetings in recent months. Ankara is also refusing or ignoring bilateral meeting requ

stories.title: Family accuse UK government of lack of support over death of Briton in Grenada
stories.summary: Pathologist found Andrew Frederick’s death was a homicide but UK authorities refused specialist help The grieving family of a British man found dead in his home on the Caribbean island of Grenada have accused UK authorities of failing to support their fight for justice. The family of Andrew Frederick, 47, whose body was discovered on 4 January, are calling for an urgent review of the policies governing UK assistance to the loved ones of Britons killed abroad. Continue reading...

stories.title: EU fines Temu for failing to stop sale of illegal and dangerous products
stories.summary: European Commission finds shoppers on Chinese website very likely to find unsafe items and imposes €200m penalty EU regulators have fined the Chinese shopping website Temu €200m (£173m) for failing to stop the sale of illegal and dangerous products. The European Commission imposed the penalty after a 19-month investigation that found consumers were very likely to encounter illegal or unsafe products including baby toys and electronics on the firm’s website. Continue reading...

stories.title: New food exports deal signals end to Brexit ‘sausage wars’
stories.summary: First details of agreement published, heralding end to EU-UK checks on dairy products, eggs, fish and fresh red meat Business live – latest updates The EU and UK have signalled an end to Brexit “sausage wars” with the first details of a new food exports agreement being published by the British government. The deal will mean no more paperwork or physical checks on dairy, fish, cheese, eggs and fresh red meat from the summer of 2027 for both British exporters to the EU and EU exporters to the UK.

stories.title: Donald Trump shares draft Iran peace agreement with Israel and other allies
stories.summary: US president’s move comes as both sides try to prevent fresh ceasefire breaches scuppering a potential deal Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has circulated a draft peace agreement for the war with Iran among allies including Israel as both sides try to prevent fresh breaches of the ceasefire escalating out of control and scuppering any deal. stories.title: Dormitory fire at Kenyan girls’ school kills at least 16 students
stories.summary: Parents face anxious wait for updates after blaze tears through Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county A fire has ripped through a dormitory at a girls’ school in Kenya’s Rift valley, killing at least 16 students. The fire broke out just after midnight at Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county, about 76 miles north-west of Nairobi, police said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tony Abbott to step down from Advance role when elected Liberal party federal president
stories.summary: Liberal insiders concerned former PM could pull party even further to the right by appointing Advance allies to prominent positions Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tony Abbott will step down from his role advising the rightwing advocacy group, Advance, as the conservative ideologue prepares for his high-profile role as the Liberal party’s new federal president . Liberal insiders have also shared concerns Abbott is considering Advance’s director, Matthew Sheahan, or S

stories.title: EU to discuss potential restrictions on Chinese imports amid fears of overreliance
stories.summary: Concerns among commissioners in bloc that surge in imports could lead to decline similar to that of US rust belt towns EU commissioners will meet on Friday for crunch talks aimed at imposing new restrictions on imports from China amid growing concern that Beijing is fuelling conditions for US-style rust belt towns in Europe. The surge in imports of everything from electric cars to key components in machines, medical devices and foodstuffs has been dubbed China Shock 2.0, potentially mirroring th

stories.title: Enfield council withdraws from government’s new towns programme
stories.summary: London authority’s new Tory-led administration delivers significant blow to Labour’s flagship housebuilding scheme Enfield council in north London has withdrawn from the government’s new towns programme, in a significant blow to Labour’s flagship housebuilding scheme. The move by the new minority Conservative-led administration could present one of the first tests of Rachel Reeves’s planning changes , designed to curb the use of judicial reviews against new infrastructure. Continue reading...

stories.title: Netanyahu orders Israeli army to seize ‘70% of Gaza Strip’, violating ceasefire deal
stories.summary: Speaking in West Bank settlement, Israeli PM, who is fighting for political survival before elections, says ‘we are squeezing Hamas’ Middle East crisis – live updates Benjamin Netanyahu has said he has given orders to the Israeli army to seize control of 70% of the Gaza Strip in a move that threatens to torpedo an already fragile ceasefire and create catastrophic humanitarian conditions in the already devastated territory. Under the US-brokered ceasefire in October, the Israeli army withdrew to

stories.title: Happy the elephant dies aged 55 after being euthanized at New York zoo
stories.summary: Happy was the focus of a high-profile court case launched in 2018 over whether she had the legal rights of a person Happy, an elephant that became embroiled in a high-profile court case over whether she had the legal rights of a person, has died after being euthanized at New York City’s Bronx zoo at the age of 55. The Asian elephant, was euthanized on Tuesday after zoo staff determined that “progressive, age-related health conditions” required the decision, according to the Wildlife Conservation

stories.title: Labour poised for fresh welfare changes after scale of youth jobs crisis revealed

Tell us: we would like to hear from young people in the UK about their job hunting experience Labour is poised for a fresh attempt at changing the welfare system after a major government-backed report said youth unemployment was costing Britain more than £125bn a year. stories.title: Dire strait? Trump’s bid to end Iran war before midterms risks Republican anger
stories.summary: Trump administration in a bind as it faces mounting economic costs ahead of midterms from Iran war The terms of a purported 60-day deal to negotiate peace in the Iran war have trapped the Trump administration between mounting economic costs ahead of midterm elections and anger from Republican hawks who accuse the US government of surrendering to Iran. The public rift between Trump and Senate Republicans over his shift toward diplomacy with Iran has also been matched within his administration, wh

stories.title: Austrian man found guilty of plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
stories.summary: An Austrian court convicts Beran A, 21, after alleged plan to attack fans outside stadium in 2024 An Austrian court has convicted a man of planning to attack a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna nearly two years ago. The state court in Wiener Neustadt on Thursday found the 21-year-old defendant, an Austrian citizen known only as Beran A – in line with Austrian privacy rules – guilty on charges including those related to the concert, the Austria Press Agency reported. Continue reading...

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran says Trump’s threats to ‘blow up’ Oman ‘dangerous and bullying’
stories.summary: US president said ally would be at risk if it did not ‘behave just like everybody else’ Hezbollah has claimed dozens of drone and rocket attacks that it said targeted Israeli troops in southern Lebanon and northern Israel. The group said it launched several attacks on Israeli soldiers and tanks that crossed the Litani river into the town of Zawtar al-Sharqiyah near Nabatieh, as close-range fighting continues. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine: EU's Kallas warns against Russian mediator 'trap'
stories.summary: The European Union's top diplomat, Kaja Kallas, has warned member states against falling into a Russian "trap" in future negotiations on Ukraine as the bloc's foreign ministers meet in Cyprus. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany: Who is Economy Minister Katherina Reiche?
stories.summary: Economy Minister Reiche is a controversial figure. Her plans for new gas-fired power plants, rolling back plans for more renewables in the building sector and scrapping solar subsidies have been met with criticism.

stories.title: Iran, US reportedly agree to deal, pending Trump approval
stories.summary: US media reported that American and Iranian negotiators have agreed to resume nuclear talks and allow shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Israel has launched strikes across Lebanon. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Hopes for peace deal fade as US and Iran exchange fire again
stories.summary: US forces targeted Iranian drones near Bandar Abbas as the IRGC claimed it struck back at a US airbase, while Washington sanctioned Tehran's Hormuz toll authority and both sides published contradictory accounts of a possible deal.

stories.title: Uganda shuts border with DR Congo as cases of rare Ebola type surge
stories.summary: The decision by the Ugandan Ebola task force, which goes against World Health Organization guidance, follows a rise in health workers exposed to Ebola by Congolese patients who crossed before the outbreak was declared.

stories.title: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
stories.summary: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

stories.title: US justice department launches criminal investigation into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll
stories.summary: The case centres on whether the former columnist lied about the funding she received for her civil lawsuit against the US president.

stories.title: Trump endorses Pashinyan ahead of Armenia’s high-stakes, historic elections
stories.summary: Trump endorsed PM Pashinyan by calling on Armenians to “Make Armenia Great Again“ ahead of the decisive June elections. The US and EU’s support for Armenia’s pro-West course and Russia's mounting economic pressure to counter Pashinyan mark the geopolitical juncture.

stories.summary: French students are taking their exams in baking schools, while red alerts are issued in Italy.

stories.title: Brussels moves to take control of its poorest municipality
stories.summary: Brussels’ regional government on Thursday announced it would move to place the municipality of Saint-Josse-ten-Noode, which borders the EU district, under mandatory supervision citing a dire budgetary state and systemic dysfunction. stories.title: EU forecasts €13B budget return from new gambling tax
stories.summary: A new EU tax on online gambling could generate €13.3 billion in fresh revenue for the bloc’s next seven-year budget , the European Commission forecast in a document seen by POLITICO. Potential levies on crypto firms and U.S. tech giants such as Google and Amazon are estimated to yield around €20 billion and €35 billion, respectively, throughout the seven-year budget cycle, according to the document shared by the EU executive with national governments and the European Parliament on Thursday. stories.title: G7 : les États-Unis refusent de céder sur l’impact environnemental de la tech et la régulation des grandes entreprises
stories.summary: PARIS — Les États-Unis ne veulent pas s’engager, dans le cadre de la déclaration commune des ministres du G7, en faveur d’une politique plus volontariste sur l’impact environnemental du secteur technologique. Alors que les ministres du Numérique du G7 doivent se réunir ce vendredi à Paris, les Etats-Unis ont fixé plusieurs lignes rouges dans les négociations. Celles-ci concernent notamment l’environnement, ont indiqué mercredi des responsables français à la presse. Washin

stories.title: Wild weather unleashes thunderstorms across south-east Australia, triggering flash flooding
stories.summary: Wet weather batters parts of Queensland, NSW and Tasmania amid warnings of heavy falls on Friday Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Wild weather more typical of summer than the last days of autumn has caused chaos in south-eastern Australia, unleashing a string of severe thunderstorms. Heavy rainfall accompanied by 500,000 lightning strikes across Queensland and New South Wales triggered widespread flash flooding, road closures and travel disruptions. Continue reading..

stories.title: Feeding the future of France: Rollout of €1 meals an attempt to help struggling students
stories.summary: It’s a thumbs up from the country’s 3 million students, who can now buy cheap meals up to twice a day Where in France can you get a nutritious and balanced three-course meal for €1? If you are one of the country’s estimated 3 million students in higher education, the answer is: the university restaurant or cafe. Continue reading...

stories.title: Blond Bangladeshi buffalo nicknamed ‘Donald Trump’ saved from Eid sacrifice
stories.summary: Rare albino buffalo spared due to security concerns over unusual level of public interest in 700kg animal A rare albino buffalo in Bangladesh nicknamed “Donald Trump” for its distinctive blond tuft has been spared from Eid al-Adha sacrifice after a last-minute government intervention, according to a home ministry official. The nearly 700kg (1,543lb) animal had already been sold for ritual slaughter when authorities stepped in, citing security concerns after a surge of public interest before Thur

stories.title: The race for oil: will Jamaica be the next country to drill and what does that mean for its green pledges?
stories.summary: With early tests suggesting the presence of crude oil, the Caribbean island has begun to debate whether it could justify becoming a producer Jamaica is closer than ever to drilling for oil. Tests on samples from the seabed off the Caribbean island’s south coast earlier this year identified hydrocarbons , which suggest the presence of crude oil below ground. Jamaica imports all its fuel, which costs about $1.5-2bn (£1.1bn-1.5bn) annually , depending on global oil prices. It is a persistent drag o

stories.title: Victorian greyhound trainer avoids ban despite dog being found with cocaine in system
stories.summary: Tribunal decision on Geoffrey Dalton comes as advocacy group releases data showing 30 greyhounds have died at or after Victorian races this year Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A veteran greyhound trainer whose dog was found to have cocaine in its system at a race has avoided a temporary ban despite the industry regulator calling for one. stories.title: ‘Hidden datacentre tax’ costing Irish households millions, report says
stories.summary: Datacentres used 22% of country’s electricity last year, pushing up household bills, study suggests Energy demand by datacentres in Ireland has added hundreds of euros to household electricity bills in a pattern that could be replicated across Europe, according to a report. Ireland’s growing number of datacentres last year used 22% of the country’s electricity, more than all urban homes combined , according to the Central Statistics Office. The equivalent figure in the US and UK is 6% . Continue

stories.title: France overturns law classing people as property – 178 years after it abolished slavery
stories.summary: National assembly votes to repeal Code Noir under which enslaved people were beaten, raped and killed For almost 180 years after France abolished slavery, the Code Noir (Black Code) allowing enslaved humans to be treated as property and worked, beaten, sold, raped or killed, remained in place. On Thursday, the country’s bitterly divided national assembly voted unanimously to repeal it, in a rare show of political unity. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK minister visits Australia for ‘lessons’ ahead of expected British social media crackdown
stories.summary: Kanishka Narayan says Australia’s pioneering law has contributed to national conversation under way in Britain The UK’s online safety minister says he has spent a week in Australia learning the “practical lessons” of the country’s under-16s social media ban amid concern that many teenagers are bypassing the law. The British government is expected to announce a social media crackdown within weeks after a public consultation that could see the UK follow in Australia’s footsteps and restrict access

stories.title: British Museum postpones Jewish culture month lecture over protest fears
stories.summary: Decision draws criticism from some commentators and free speech advocates as well as Tory leader, Kemi Badenoch The British Museum has postponed a lecture for Jewish culture month over concerns that the event would be disrupted by protesters. The museum announced that the talk on ancient Israel and Judah, which was scheduled to take place on Thursday, would be held at a later date. Continue reading...

stories.title: Image of Thai police in sparkly dresses with handcuffed suspect turns out to be AI fake
stories.summary: Picture was created by administrator in charge of station’s Facebook account who wanted to create ‘friendlier image’ It was an arresting image and an irresistible story. A group of tough Thai police officers – five men and one woman – all wearing elaborate festival-style dresses, surrounding a drug dealer they had caught while undercover. The image, released by local police, was so compelling that it found its way on to the front page of the UK’s Daily Star, as well as in picture stories in the

stories.title: Much of what Blair said in essay criticising Labour was wrong, says Starmer – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here Pat McFadden , the work and pensions secretary, is introducing Alan Milburn. He says Milburn’s report is “really important and powerful”. I could see in the first few weeks after being appointed as the secretary of state what was happening, both in human and in financial terms, [in terms of youth unemployment]. And I knew that we had to get properly under the bonnet of this problem, because there’s a lot more thing than one thing happ

stories.title: Milan restores bull mosaic’s testicles worn down by pirouetting tourists
stories.summary: The artwork in a 19th-century shopping arcade has been damaged by visitors honouring an unusual tradition A floor mosaic of an anatomically detailed bull in one of Milan’s grand arcades is getting a sensitive makeover after being worn down by thousands of passersby honouring an unusual tradition. Legend has it that grinding your heel on the bull’s testicles at the Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II guarantees you will return to the city. Continue reading...

stories.title: Keir Starmer defends policy choices in rebuttal of Blair’s criticism
stories.summary: PM says predecessor misunderstands government’s successes and ‘very different’ situation compared with 1997 Keir Starmer has dismissed Tony’s Blair’s argument that his government is on the wrong track, saying he is implementing the policies needed for today, not the very different situation faced by the former prime minister in 1997. “You won’t be surprised to know that I don’t agree with much that Tony says about what the government is doing,” Starmer said during a visit to an apprentice traini

stories.title: Carney calls for new US-Canada partnership to ‘help make America great again’
stories.summary: Canada prime minister urges greater economic cooperation between the two countries in speech delivered in New York Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney has called for a new relationship with the United States to “help make America great again”. In a speech delivered in New York on Thursday, Carney said that there should be a “true partnership” that reimagines cooperation in specific sectors challenged by global competition. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Hundreds of job applications’: young people on their struggle to find work
stories.summary: Job seekers say the lack of opportunities has affected their mental health and left them fearing for the future When Catherina finished her degree in digital film production in London, she thought her prospects of finding work were good, but she has found the jobs market tough. Continue reading...

stories.title: Clashes between armed groups in Colombia kill at least 52
stories.summary: Rival groups are vying for territorial control of strategic cocaine production and trafficking region At least 52 guerrilla fighters have been killed in clashes between two rival armed groups vying for territorial control of a strategic cocaine production and trafficking region in south-east Colombia, a faction of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc) involved in the fighting has said. The clashes, the most violent in recent months, took place in the jungles of the department of Guav

stories.title: Greener pasture of a shepherd’s life lures Chinese workers penned in by ‘996’ jobs
stories.summary: When an Inner Mongolia farmer sought two herders to tend his 3,000 sheep, he was swamped with applicants including graduates, factory labourers and white-collar workers A Chinese farm owner’s recruitment drive for shepherds has ended in success after his job advert seeking people to work on his Inner Mongolia ranch went viral, drawing the attention of city dwellers struggling to find work and highlighting growing strains in China’s labour market. Zuo Xiaoyong posted an advert on Chinese social m

stories.title: Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security
stories.summary: Strategic petroleum reserves are declining rapidly as countries release emergency crude supplies to cushion the economic impact of the Middle East conflict.

stories.title: New Ebola cases in Congo: What you need to know
stories.summary: The virus spreads from direct person-to-person contact. But here's what makes it especially lethal: it persists in corpses, and funeral practices often take place precisely when bodies are most infectious.

stories.title: US: Former CIA officer charged with stealing gold bars from government
stories.summary: A former CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing gold bars worth millions from the US government and stashing them in his home. Investigators also seized foreign currency and luxury watches.

stories.title: India news: Siddaramaiah steps down as Karnataka CM
stories.summary: Main headlines in India include DK Shivkumar being set to replace Siddaramaiah as chief minister for the southern state Karnataka, and Muslims celebrating Eid al-Adha across the country. DW has more.

stories.title: A look inside Japan's largest prison for women
stories.summary: Japan has recently carried out reforms to its prison system, but inside Tochigi, its biggest women's prison, strict discipline and tough conditions still define daily life.

stories.title: North Korea: New report sheds light on chemical weapons
stories.summary: A new study shows the North Korean regime is investing heavily in chemical weapons capacity, with analysts warning they could be deployed by the regime if it faces an existential threat.

stories.title: Ethiopia votes amid geopolitical rivalries in Horn of Africa
stories.summary: Ethiopia will hold its seventh national election on June 1, 2026. The vote takes place as Gulf states compete for influence in the region and the rivalry between Ethiopia and Egypt over the Nile intensifies.

stories.title: Why Europe is the fastest-warming continent
stories.summary: Europe is sweltering under an early heat wave that has broken records and claimed lives. What is happening to make it so hot?

stories.title: UK re-chalks Dorset's famed and feisty Cerne Abbas Giant
stories.summary: Britain's National Trust is working on replacing the chalk outline of the Cerne Abbas Giant in Dorset. The Saxon-era carving depicts a club-wielding man "still standing tall," as the charity puts it.

stories.title: Iran turns internet back on, but old restrictions remain
stories.summary: Communication with the outside world remains difficult in Iran, even after an 88-day total internet blockade was lifted. The blackout has had significant economic and social consequences.

stories.title: Kai Havertz: Arsenal and Germany's man for the big moment
stories.summary: Germany forward Kai Havertz is set to play in his second Champions League final. The Arsenal man is a match winner.

stories.title: Crises in Amhara, Tigray cloud Ethiopia's election 2026
stories.summary: The June 1 elections come as the country debates federalism, governance and national unity in polls that could define the country's democratic future.

stories.title: Earth on track for record heat over next 5 years — UN report
stories.summary: Meteorologists predict a high chance that global average temperatures could reach record levels between 2026-2030.

stories.title: The Suez and Panama canals charge tolls. Why is Iran’s Hormuz fee triggering global outrage?
stories.summary: Iran has been widely condemned for demanding up to $2 million per transit through the Strait of Hormuz. DW explores the reasons why Egypt can charge for the Suez Canal and Panama for its waterway, but not Tehran.

stories.title: France moves to symbolically repeal slavery legislation
stories.summary: Members of the lower house of the French parliament unanimously voted to abolish a series of royal edicts known as the "Code noir" or Black Code. The bill stops short of some lawmakers' demands like possible reparations.

stories.title: EU fines Temu €200M over unsafe toys, non-compliant products
stories.summary: EU regulators said Temu failed to identify and assess the systemic risks of illegal products' on its platform and the harm they posed to consumers.

stories.title: UFC fight venue takes shape outside White House for Trump birthday event
stories.summary: Construction has begun on a UFC octagon outside the White House ahead of a fight night linked to Donald Trump’s 80th birthday celebrations and the 250th anniversary of the United States.

stories.title: Viral ‘Donald Trump’ buffalo spared from Eid sacrifice and sent to zoo
stories.summary: Bangladesh's national zoo has taken custody of a rare albino buffalo nicknamed "Donald Trump", sparing the animal from sacrifice during Eid al-Adha celebrations.

stories.title: Dalai Lama attends swearing‑in of Tibetan government‑in‑exile in India
stories.summary: Penpa Tsering, the head of Tibet’s government‑in‑exile, was sworn in for a second term at a ceremony in Dharamshala, India, attended by the Dalai Lama.

stories.title: Fire rips through dormitory at girl's school in Kenya, killing at least 16 students
stories.summary: There have been many devastating school fires in Kenya, where boarding schools are common as a colonial legacy of missionaries and British rule.

stories.title: New Russian law allows bank employees to take down Ukrainian drones
stories.summary: Russia moves to arm bank staff with electronic warfare gear against Ukrainian drones, in a sign its military defences are struggling amid its all-out war against Ukraine, now in its fifth year.

stories.title: Plans for Gaza International Stabilisation Force in question as troop pledges stall
stories.summary: The Iran war has made it more difficult for Arab and Muslim leaders to openly cooperate with the United States and Israel, which many in the region view as aggressors.

stories.title: Switzerland: Footage from the scene emerge after Winterthur station stabbing injures three
stories.summary: The first images from Winterthur station show police and paramedics securing the platforms, treating the injured and evacuating passengers as trains are halted and the area is cordoned off.

stories.title: Armenia holds first military parade in a decade on Republic Day
stories.summary: Armenia marked Republic Day on 28 May with a military parade in Yerevan, the first such event held in the country since 2016.

stories.title: Oil prices fall after report of breakthrough in US-Iran talks
stories.summary: A report of a extended ceasefire, subject to Donald Trump's approval, has led to global oil prices to fall on Thursday.

stories.title: Villas, cars and cash: Italy seizes dead Mafia mobster's millions
stories.summary: Police say the operation targeted the network of notorious late Sicilian mafia boss Matteo Messina Denaro.

stories.title: EU fines Temu €200m for allowing sale of illegal products
stories.summary: The European Commission says the Chinese-owned online retailer failed to take account of risks from baby toys and faulty chargers sold on its platform.

stories.title: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.title: Judge refuses to block Trump order to create federal voter list and limit mail voting
stories.summary: Democrats and civil rights groups argued it was urgent that the judge issue a restraining order in the midst of primary season and with states already gearing up for the fall midterm elections.

stories.title: Man arrested after three injured in stabbing at Swiss train station
stories.summary: A 31-year-old Swiss man has been arrested, police say, as images from the scene show parts of the station cordoned off.

stories.title: Questions over safety as 16 pupils die in another Kenya school fire
stories.summary: Several parents were still waiting for news of their children roughly 12 hours after the fire.

stories.title: Are US and Iran close to peace or sliding back to war?
stories.summary: Neither side seems interested in a return to all-out conflict, despite the latest exchange of strikes.

stories.title: Israel hits Lebanese capital in 'targeted strike'
stories.summary: Beirut had until now largely been spared even as both Israel and Hezbollah accused each other of breaking last month's ceasefire.

stories.title: US and Iran reach tentative deal, pending Trump's approval
stories.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to extend the ceasefire by 60 days to make space for further negotiations to end the war but Trump still has to approve the deal, reports said Thursday, citing US officials familiar with the matter.

stories.title: Israel freezes out UN chief António Guterres over inclusion on sexual violence list
stories.summary: The UN cited "credible information" regarding sexual violence allegedly committed by Israeli security forces against Palestinians in prisons and other detention centres.

stories.title: What are the Abraham Accords — and why is Trump pushing to expand them?
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump is calling for more Muslim-majority countries to join the Abraham Accords, the diplomatic agreements normalising ties between Israel and parts of the Arab world.

stories.title: Man jailed for 15 years over plot to attack Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
stories.summary: A 21-year-old Austrian man admitted planning the attack and other terrorism-related offences.

stories.title: Trump warns Iran to make a deal or 'we'll finish the job'
stories.summary: Speaking at a White House cabinet meeting on Wednesday, the US president said he was unsatisfied with Tehran's offers and in no rush, even as the White House dismissed an Iranian state television report of a draft agreement as a "complete fabrication".

stories.title: Australia sues US giant 3M over 'forever chemicals' in firefighting foam
stories.summary: The A$2bn case, which centres on contamination at defence sites, is the largest ever brought by the government.

stories.title: US military conducts second strike against Iran within days as Trump fumes over negotiations
stories.summary: According to the US Central Command, its forces struck four Iranian one-way attack drones posing a threat around the Strait of Hormuz and a ground control station in Bandar Abbas 'that was about to launch a fifth drone'.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian strike injures 11 in Odesa daytime attack, children among injured
stories.summary: Russian daytime strike hits the southern port city of Odesa on 27 May, injuring at least 11 including children, and igniting a huge fire that ravages shops and commercial buildings.

stories.title: New head of Hamas' military wing killed in strike on Gaza, Israel says
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office described Odeh as one of the "architects" of the October 7 attacks.

stories.title: Thousands in Georgia rally for EU membership as country marks 35th independence anniversary
stories.summary: Waving Georgian and EU flags, thousands of protesters marched through central Tbilisi before staging a rally outside parliament amid a heavy police presence.

stories.title: In court as Matthew Perry's assistant sentenced

stories.title: How my brother went from liberal Hollywood actor to manosphere 'messiah'
stories.summary: The sister of Mexican influencer El Temach tells the BBC she does not recognise him any more.

stories.title: Is 'out of control' US tipping culture spreading overseas?
stories.summary: With US waiting staff getting cross at receiving less than 20%, tips are also on the rise elsewhere.

stories.title: Google worker charged with using internal data to make $1.2m on bets
stories.summary: The longtime Google employee was charged in New York for allegedly breaking insider trading laws.

stories.title: Duterte ICC trial to start on 30 November: What you need to know

stories.title: Australia charges woman who returned from Syria with joining Islamic State

stories.title: Jill Biden says she thought husband was having a stroke during 2024 debate
stories.summary: The former US first lady told CBS News that she was frightened by Joe Biden's performance against Donald Trump.

stories.title: Ireland records 30C while Portugal nears 37C in May heat
stories.summary: A record May heatwave sent Irish temperatures above 30C and Portugal’s to 37C, drawing crowds outdoors and renewing worries over climate change.

stories.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said earlier on Thursday that its forces had targeted a US airbase.

stories.title: Iranians back online after months-long shutdown but heavy restrictions still remain
stories.summary: Iranian authorities decided to lift some restrictions this week as negotiators appeared to be closing in on a more permanent truce, but many Iranians now fear access could be cut off again, at a moment’s notice.

stories.title: France heat triggers illegal swimming in central Paris
stories.summary: France's exceptional May heat continued on 27 May as temperatures climbed around 33C in Paris. Many residents headed to the Saint-Martin canal to cool off, despite rules prohibiting swimming in the waterway.

stories.title: Felhőparádéval érkezett Siófokra tegnap a vihar
stories.summary: Nem ufótámadás, nem kísérleti atomrobbantás, egy igazán impozáns zivatarfelhő látható a HungaroMet fotóján.

stories.title: Képernyő helyett élményekre vágynak az európaiak

A Mastercard 28 országból 27 ezer embert kérdezett meg: a kutatás rámutatott arra, hogy a válaszadóknak elegük van az algoritmusokból, a folyamatos értesítésekből és az állandó digitális jelenlétből.

stories.summary: Az államtitkár azt is elmondta, hogy három helyettes segíti majd a munkáját.

stories.title: Mérgező játék babák, veszélyes töltők: 70 milliárd forintra büntette a Temut az EU
stories.summary: Az Európai Bizottság szerint a Temu nem figyel oda arra, hogy mennyi illegális termék kapható a platformon.

stories.title: Több helyszínen is razziáztak a nyomozók az MNB-ügyben
stories.summary: A Központi Nyomozó Főügyészség azt ígérte, pénteken beszámolnak a nyomozás fejleményeiről.

stories.summary: Báró Krasznahorkai hazatér, Tamás Gáspár Miklós után kongó csend marad, egy Suzuki Swift rendszám pedig az iszapba esik. Nézni- hallgatni- és olvasnivalók csütörtöktől csütörtökig.

stories.title: Algoritmusok helyett még mindig élőben a legjobb új zenéket felfedezni
stories.summary: Pénteken várunk mindenkit a Telex After bulijára a Kassa Hajón, ahol lesz vad garázsrock, szerzői elektronika és absztrakt hiphop is.

stories.summary: Pósfai Gábor belügyminiszter arról posztolt, hogy a rendőrök 14 helyen tartottak házkutatást, 120 embert hallgattak ki.

Az egész nemcsak a demokraták között keltett felzúdulást, hanem nem kevés republikánus szenátort is feldühített.

stories.title: Felmondta a Népszava szerződéseit a Mediaworks, már a pénteki lapszám sem jelenhet meg
stories.summary: A Népszava jelentős tartozásokat halmozott fel a Fidesz-közeli médiaholding tulajdonában álló nyomda és terjesztő cég felé.

stories.title: Annyi autósra számít a rendőrség a BL-döntő miatt, hogy inkább rendkívüli kamionstopot rendeltek el

stories.title: Meghalt a négyszeres Stanley Kupa-győztes volt jégkorongozó, Claude Lemieux
stories.summary: A korábbi kanadai jégkorongozó hatvanéves volt.

stories.title: Senkinek nem jó, ha nem lehet behajtani a tartozásokat – nem ellenségképekre van szükség a követeléskezelés reformjában
stories.summary: A szakma szerint is felül kell vizsgálni a végrehajtási eljárás rendszerét és szabályait, de azt jó tudni, hogy hatékony követeléskezelés nélkül működő piacgazdaság és jogbiztonság.

stories.title: How the plastic bottle cap became a parable for the value of EU regulation | Alberto Alemanno
stories.summary: Supporters of deregulation want Europe to be more like the US. But that would serve only American interests In July 2024, a European Union law came into force requiring plastic bottle caps to remain attached to their bottles. The regulation was widely mocked by social-media jokesters and Silicon Valley billionaires alike. This, people said, was Brussels at its worst: bureaucrats micromanaging, treating citizens like children who couldn’t be trusted to recycle a cap. What went almost entirely unr

stories.title: Brexiters peddled ‘nationalistic pish’, said Reform UK’s Makerfield candidate
stories.summary: Robert Kenyon castigated Brexit as an economically self-harming project on rugby league forum in 2016 Reform UK’s candidate for the Makerfield byelection has castigated Brexit as an economically self-harming project promoted by politicians who “peddled the nationalistic pish”, raising more doubts about his commitment to Nigel Farage’s signature achievement. The comments by Robert Kenyon, unearthed on a defunct rugby league forum and first reported by the Telegraph, follow the emergence of anothe

stories.title: Turkish police fire water cannon at protesters after opposition leader ousted – video
stories.summary: Riot police in Turkey have fired a water cannon and teargas to break up a rally called by the ousted opposition leader Özgür Özel days after a court dismissed him from office. Özel called the lunchtime rally in İzmir as Turkey was poised to shut down for the four-day Eid al-Fitr holiday, which begins on Wednesday. On Sunday, riot police had battered their way into the main opposition CHP’s headquarters in the capital, Ankara, firing teargas and beating party members before throwing them out, Öze

stories.title: Spain: Police raid HQ of ruling PSOE Socialist party in graft probe
stories.summary: Police have searched the headquarters of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's PSOE party investigating possible illegal payments. Sanchez meanwhile said he was still standing by former PM Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero.

stories.title: Turkey: Leadership dispute engulfs opposition CHP
stories.summary: The top figures in Turkey's main opposition party differ on how to resolve the leadership vacuum after a court annulled its 2023 primary. One wants the party delegates to pick a leader, the other wants members to vote.

stories.title: IRGC warns Iran's coast will become a 'graveyard' if US strikes resume
stories.summary: An IRGC Navy official threatened to turn Iran's entire Gulf coastline into a killing ground if the US resumed strikes, even as negotiators reported the two sides were closer to an initial agreement than at any point since the April ceasefire.

stories.title: Hotel that refused to give tourist tap water acted lawfully, Italian court rules
stories.summary: The Italian Supreme Court rejected a tourist's claim that her consumer rights were breached when she was only offered €7 bottled mineral water.

stories.title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
stories.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

stories.title: Indians protest over ‘forever chemicals’ after relocation of scandal-hit Italian factory
stories.summary: Lack of Pfas regulations raised in parliament after Guardian revealed former Miteni plant bought by Indian company Protests over the production of cancer-linked Pfas chemicals have spread across India, after an investigation revealed that an Italian factory shut down due to an environmental scandal was bought by an Indian company and partly rebuilt. At the end of last year, the Guardian revealed that the former Miteni plant in Vicenza had been acquired by the Indian company Laxmi Organic Industr

stories.title: Putin offers debt relief to new recruits for Ukraine war
stories.summary: New recruits for Russia's war in Ukraine will have debts up to 10 million rubles canceled under a presidential decree. Another new law reportedly foresees the use of Russian armed forces to protect citizens abroad.

stories.title: ‘Mind-bogglingly crazy’: climate experts alarmed by deadly spring heatwaves searing Europe
stories.summary: Scientists warn of ‘new reality’ of heat extremes that claim three times more lives than car crashes and 16 times as many as murderers Malcolm Mistry knew it was going to get “very warm, very quickly” on Monday morning but a slow start out of bed delayed his plans for an early game of cricket with his son. It was already 10am by the time the pair arrived at the sun-soaked nets of their local club in south-west London, and to the embarrassment of the 48-year-old scientist, who played cricket in h

stories.title: WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warns of ‘catastrophic collision of disease and conflict’, as Uganda closes border with DRC The head of the World Health Organization has called for an immediate ceasefire in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo to help tackle the Ebola outbreak there, as Uganda closed its border with its neighbour in an effort to stop the spread. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus posted on social media that the region was in the midst of a “catastrophic collision of disease and confl

stories.title: New Mandelson revelations cast doubt on claim vetting decision was borderline, Thornberry says – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here Zack Polanski , the Green party leader, posted this on social media about Tony Blair’s latest intervention this morning. Tony Blair. What the billionaire class have paid for. Spot the difference between “Tony Blair says” and “Nigel Farage says” Continue reading...

stories.title: US building Ebola quarantine center in Kenya for Americans amid outbreak
stories.summary: Some experts criticize White House approach and say not allowing Americans to return to US hurts treatment efforts The Trump administration is building a quarantine and treatment center in Kenya for Americans affected by the Ebola outbreak, instead of bringing them home. The White House on Wednesday confirmed that the US was setting up a facility in Kenya for Americans to quarantine after Ebola exposure in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). Continue reading...

stories.title: The German gym fighting the far right with MMA
stories.summary: The far right is increasingly using MMA to recruit new members. But a gym in Chemnitz, in the east of Germany, is fighting back with a martial arts program focused on diversity and empowerment.

stories.title: License to play: The new James Bond video game
stories.summary: James Bond is back, and he's as charismatic and daring as ever. The video game "007 First Light" tells the origin story of the British secret agent — and the stakes are high.

stories.title: Germany eyes heat pumps as Iran war drives energy costs up
stories.summary: The war in the Middle East is threatening fossil fuel supplies and causing energy prices to rise in Germany. Could that drive a surge in demand for heat pumps?

stories.title: Europe's energy problem isn't green power — it's storage
stories.summary: Solar and wind produce lots of energy — but not always at the right time. More battery storage could help Europe to stabilize prices and replace polluting fossil fuel energy, but roadblocks remain.

stories.title: EU enlargement: Is the bloc ready to welcome new members?
stories.summary: Brussels says the EU must grow, but resistance within the bloc remains strong, with some arguing that it must reform before enlarging. Montenegro remains the only country with a realistic chance of joining soon.

stories.title: Germany: No recovery in sight for the economy
stories.summary: The war in Iran has dashed hopes for economic growth. Germany's pension and healthcare systems are also feeling the strain.

stories.title: FIFA World Cup: New York and New Jersey prosecutors probe 'sky-high' ticket prices
stories.summary: US prosecutors have subpoenaed FIFA over expensive World Cup tickets, saying the event is "not an invitation to exploit our residents and visitors."

stories.title: Watch: UFC arena construction begins at White House ahead of fight
stories.summary: The massive structure will host a cage fight as part of celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

stories.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
stories.summary: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

stories.title: Israel expands military ground operations in Southern Lebanon as clashes with Hezbollah intensify
stories.summary: Israel’s military said it struck more than 100 Hezbollah sites across southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley area overnight.

stories.title: Ukraine’s drone commander warns Lukashenka about 500 potential targets in Belarus
stories.summary: Amid rising tensions with Minsk, Ukrainian military commander issued a warning to Aliaksandr Lukashenka and warned him against Belarus' deeper involvement n Russia’s all-out war.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV tests Ferrari’s electric future with new Luce supercar
stories.summary: Ferrari presented its first fully electric car, the Ferrari Luce, to Pope Leo XIV during a meeting at Castel Gandolfo on 26 May.

stories.title: Thousands rally in Tbilisi as Georgia marks Independence Day with pro-EU protests
stories.summary: Protesters in Tbilisi denounce Georgian Dream, accusing it of stalling EU accession, eroding civil liberties and pushing the country towards Russia.

stories.title: In war-torn Gaza, Palestinians mark Eid al-Adha among the rubble
stories.summary: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip marked Eid al-Adha on 27 May under the shadow of war, gathering for prayers in heavily damaged areas of Khan Younis and Gaza City.

stories.title: Greenpeace stages record deep-sea protest against seabed mining
stories.summary: Greenpeace used a robot more than 2,300 metres below the ocean surface to unfurl a “Listen to the Science” banner in protest against deep-sea mining.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia: More than 1.5 million pilgrims perform stoning ritual in Mina
stories.summary: More than 1.5 million Muslim pilgrims gathered in Mina, Saudi Arabia, on 27 May to perform the symbolic stoning ritual that marks a key stage of the annual Hajj pilgrimage.

stories.title: Five of seven people trapped in flooded Laos cave for more than a week found alive, rescuers say
stories.summary: The cave is located in a rugged, remote area in Xaisomboun province’s Longcheng district, about 120 kilometres north of the capital, Vientiane.

stories.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
stories.summary: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.

stories.title: Trump scores a trophy in Texas as he hunts down Republican rivals
stories.summary: The primary defeat of long-time Senator John Cornyn confirms Trump still has a grip on the party, but comes with huge political risks – and Democrats are excited.
